studyname: Evaluation of the Impact of a Language Model-Based Tool for Automated Test Generation in Unity Projects
temp_message: Aviso: Este sitio web está disponible exclusivamente para la revisión y evaluación por parte del Comité de Ética en Investigación de la UFRN. Ninguna respuesta enviada a través de esta página será almacenada ni utilizada con fines de investigación.
rsc-plugin: You are invited to participate in the academic research study “Evaluation of the Impact of a Language Model-Based Tool for Automated Test Generation in Unity Projects”, conducted by Esdras Caleb Oliveira Silva (principal investigator), Lyrene Fernandes da Silva (supervisor), and Roberta de Souza Coelho (co-supervisor), from the Federal University of Rio Grande do Norte (UFRN).

This study is intended for game developers, software engineers, students, and independent (indie) developers aged 18 years or older who use the Unity Engine. Its objective is to evaluate how the "Laundry & Dishes" tool influences the adoption of automated testing and the developer experience, contributing to the improvement of Artificial Intelligence-assisted testing tools.

If you agree to participate, the plugin may share operational telemetry data generated during its use. Telemetry sharing is disabled by default and will only occur after your explicit consent. Event logs are stored locally on your computer and remain entirely under your control.

Information that may be shared:
Random Installation ID; Unity version; Laundry & Dishes version; features used; execution mode (local or cloud language model); test generation or execution results; anonymous target element identifier; date and time of operations.

Information that is never collected:
Source code; project names; file or asset names; file paths; API keys; prompts sent to language models; passwords; personal files; or any personally identifiable information.

Risks and Confidentiality:
The risks are minimal and are mainly related to the Internet transmission of anonymized operational telemetry data and the potential temporary increase in processing, memory, or storage usage when running language models locally. The Installation ID is a randomly generated identifier used solely to distinguish one installation of the tool from another and cannot be used to identify its user. The research team has no access to logs stored locally unless you explicitly choose to share them. Shared data will be stored in a secure institutional environment with access restricted to the responsible researchers for five years after the end of the study. Results will be published only in aggregated or anonymized form, making participant identification impossible.

Participant Rights:
Participation is voluntary. You may enable or disable telemetry sharing at any time without any penalty. Once disabled, no new data will be transmitted to the researchers. You may also request the deletion of previously shared data associated with your Installation ID. Locally stored logs remain entirely under your control and may be deleted by you at any time.

Benefits:
There is no direct benefit to participants. The expected benefit is that the results of this study will contribute to the development of more effective automated test generation tools for developers using the Unity Engine.

Participation in this research involves no costs or financial compensation. Should any damage be demonstrably caused by participation in this study, participants will be entitled to the rights provided under applicable Brazilian legislation.

If you have any questions, please contact:
Esdras Caleb Oliveira Silva – SEDIS/UFRN
Email: esdrascaleb@ufrn.br
Phone: +55 (84) 99688-8803

Ethical oversight is provided by the Research Ethics Committee of the Federal University of Rio Grande do Norte (CEP Central/UFRN):
Email: cepufrn@propesq.ufrn.br
WhatsApp: +55 (84) 99193-6266

You may also contact the Brazilian National Research Ethics Commission (CONEP):
Phone: +55 (61) 3315-5877
Email: conep@saude.gov.br

consent.title: Informed Consent Record (ICR)
consent.helper: If any term is unclear, try reading in another language.
paragraphs: You are invited to participate in the academic research study “Evaluation of the Impact of a Language Model-Based Tool for Automated Test Generation in Unity Projects”, conducted by Esdras Caleb Oliveira Silva (principal investigator), Lyrene Fernandes da Silva (supervisor), and Roberta de Souza Coelho (co-supervisor), from the Federal University of Rio Grande do Norte (UFRN).

This study is intended for game developers, software engineers, students, and independent (indie) developers aged 18 years or older who use the Unity Engine. Its objective is to evaluate how the "Laundry & Dishes" tool influences the adoption of automated testing and the developer experience. The expected outcome is to contribute to the development of tools that facilitate test automation in Unity projects.

If you agree to participate, you will complete an online questionnaire consisting of 23 questions regarding your professional profile, testing practices, and experience with the tool. The estimated completion time is approximately 10 minutes. Optionally, you may provide the tool's Installation ID to allow correlation between your questionnaire responses and voluntarily shared telemetry data, as well as an email address if you wish to be contacted for future studies related to this project.

Risks and Confidentiality:
The risks are minimal and are mainly related to possible fatigue while completing the questionnaire. Providing your email address is optional. If provided, it will be stored in a database separate from the questionnaire responses, with no direct link between them, making it impossible to associate your identity with your responses. The email address will be used exclusively for contact regarding future studies related to this project. When provided, the Installation ID is merely a random identifier associated with the software installation and cannot be used to identify the participant. Research data will be stored in a secure environment with access restricted to the responsible researchers for five years after the completion of the study. Results will be published only in aggregated or anonymized form.

Right to Withdraw:
Participation is voluntary. You may withdraw your consent and discontinue participation at any time without any penalty. You may also request the deletion of your shared data by contacting the research team.

Participation in this research involves no costs or financial compensation. Should any damage be demonstrably caused by participation in this study, participants will be entitled to the rights provided under applicable legislation.

If you have any questions, please contact:
Esdras Caleb Oliveira Silva – SEDIS/UFRN
Email: esdrascaleb@ufrn.br
Phone: +55 (84) 99688-8803

Ethical oversight is provided by the Research Ethics Committee of the Federal University of Rio Grande do Norte (CEP Central/UFRN):
Email: cepufrn@propesq.ufrn.br
WhatsApp: +55 (84) 99193-6266

You may also contact the Brazilian National Research Ethics Commission (CONEP):
Phone: +55 (61) 3315-5877
Email: conep@saude.gov.br
consent.agree: I declare that I have read this document and voluntarily agree to participate in this research study

switcher.share_survey_language: Share the language the survey was answered in

thankyou.title: Thank you for your time
thankyou.text: The survey has ended. We appreciate your attention.
thankyou.removal: If you wish to remove your data, please contact esdrascaleb@ufrn.br and send your unique ID: {{uid}}.

common.required_one_option: Please select at least one option.

survey.any_comment: Would you like to leave any additional comments?
survey.email: If you are interested in participating in future research related to this project, please provide your email address:
survey.email_contato: Leave your email if you are willing to be contacted for an interview about testing in game development.
survey.install_id: Installation ID (Unity: Edit → Project Settings → Laundry & Dishes → Installation ID). If you used more than one installation, please provide all Installation IDs separated by commas.
survey.project_used: Types of projects where the tool was used
survey.automated_testing_experience: Before this study, what was your experience with automated testing?
survey.cloud_models: Which cloud AI models did you use together with the tool?
survey.local_models: If you used a local AI model, which one did you use?
survey.easy_to_use: The tool was easy to use.
survey.helped_create_tests: The tool helped me create automated tests.
survey.tests_required_few_changes: The generated tests met my needs with few modifications.
survey.write_more_tests: After using the tool, I started writing more automated tests.
survey.encouraged_pipeline: The tool encouraged me to incorporate automated testing into my development pipeline.
survey.helped_find_bugs: The generated tests helped identify defects.
survey.continue_using: I intend to continue using the tool.
survey.recommend_tool: I would recommend the tool to other developers.
survey.software_engineering_areas: In which Software Engineering areas would you like the tool to provide support?
survey.bugs_found_count: Approximately how many defects were identified?
survey.bugs_found_example: Could you briefly describe one example?
survey.why_useful: Why would these features be useful?
survey.greatest_benefit: What was the greatest benefit provided by the tool?
survey.greatest_problem: What was the biggest problem you encountered while using the tool?
survey.missing_feature: Was there any important feature that you felt was missing?
survey.what_would_change: What would you change in the tool?
